questions.objective_ref: LO1 — Distinguish between a parameter (placeholder in definition) and an argument (actual value at call)
questions.stem_summary: Identify which term is the parameter and which is the argument in a greet() example

questions.objective_ref: LO2 — Write a function that accepts one or more positional parameters
questions.stem_summary: Predict output of two calls to describe_student() where one call passes arguments in the wrong order

questions.objective_ref: LO3 — Write a function that uses the return keyword to send a value back to the caller
questions.stem_summary: Explain what the return keyword does when Python reaches it inside a function

questions.objective_ref: LO4 — Assign the value returned by a function to a variable and use it in further code
questions.stem_summary: Identify what happens when a return value is not assigned to a variable

questions.objective_ref: LO5 — Explain what Python produces when a function has no return statement (implicit None)
questions.stem_summary: Predict what print(result) displays after result = show_greeting('Alice') where show_greeting only prints

questions.objective_ref: LO6 — Identify when a function should return a value versus when it only needs to print
questions.stem_summary: Explain why using print inside a function instead of return does not let the caller capture a useful value
